title: Feature-engineering implementation hardening
summary: Add strict checks that every engineered feature is row-aligned, target-free, and computed from allowed current or past information before model fitting.
rationale: Many failed run variants came from target references, stale submissions, pandas MultiIndex misalignment, and non-boolean masks. Preventing invalid experiments is a reusable research improvement, especially for peer and rolling feature families.
implementation_hint: Separate y before preprocessing; forbid PitNextLap, split markers, and row ids in feature transforms; assert row count and row order after preprocessing; check duplicate column names; use groupby.transform or explicit reindexing for rolling and leave-one-out features; and fail fast before training if any invalid column appears.
expected_effect: Large improvement in experiment validity and throughput; indirect AUC impact comes from making previously failure-prone ideas testable.
risk: Guards can be overly conservative and block legitimate target encodings if they are not implemented inside the fold-training loop. Keep target encoding in a separate fold-safe transformer stage.
